title: The Love Child
content:
Where the bridge out at Woodley did stride,
Wi' his wide arches' cool sheï¿½ded bow,
Up above the clear brook that did slide
By the poppies, befoam'd white as snow;
As the gilcups did quiver among
The white deï¿½sies, a-spread in a sheet.
There a quick-trippï¿½n maï¿½d come along,
Aye, a girl wi' her light-steppï¿½n veet.


An' she cried "I do praï¿½, is the road
Out to Lincham on here, by the meï¿½d?"
An' "oh! ees," I meï¿½de answer, an' show'd
Her the way it would turn an' would leï¿½d:
"Goo along by the beech in the nook,
Where the children do plaï¿½ in the cool,
To the steppï¿½n stwones over the brook,
Aye, the grey blocks o' rock at the pool."


"Then you don't seem a-born an' a-bred,"
I spoke up, "at a place here about;"
And she answer'd wi' cheï¿½ks up so red
As a pi'ny leï¿½te a-come out,
"No, I liv'd wi' my uncle that died
Back in Eï¿½pril, an' now I'm a-come
Here to Ham, to my mother, to bide,
Aye, to her house to vind a new hwome."


I'm asheï¿½med that I wanted know
Any more of her childhood or life
But then, why should so feï¿½ir a child grow
Where no father did bide wi' his wife;
Then wi' blushes of zunrisï¿½n morn,
She replied "that it midden be known,
"Oh! they zent me awaï¿½ to be born,
Aye, they hid me when some would be shown."


Oh! it meï¿½de me a'most teary-ey'd,
An' I vound I a'most could ha' groan'd
What! so winnï¿½n, an' still cast azide
What! so lovely, an' not to be own'd;
Oh! a God-gift a-treated wi' scorn
Oh! a child that a squier should own;
An' to zend her awaï¿½ to be born!
Aye, to hide her where others be shown!
meaning:
About: William Barnes (22nd February 1801 - 7th October 1886) was an English writer, poet, minister, and philologist. 
He wrote over 800 poems, some in Dorset dialect and much other work including a comprehensive English grammar quoting from more than 70 different languages.
